title: Audio: Seven Lines of Code — The Complete Pipeline

cues.label: High-level map
cues.headline: A raw image file becomes a model-ready tensor in seven lines
cues.narration: A raw image file becomes a model-ready tensor in exactly seven lines of PIL and NumPy — the goal is a (1, 3, 896, 896) float32 tensor for the Gemma 4 vision encoder.

cues.label: Analogy
cues.headline: Think of it as a seven-station assembly line
cues.narration: Think of the pipeline as a seven-station assembly line: open, resize, arrayify, floatify, normalize, permute, and batch — each station does exactly one job, then hands off.

cues.label: Tiny example
cues.headline: Follow one tiny image through every step
cues.narration: Follow one tiny image through all seven steps and watch its shape, dtype, and value range change at every row of the table.

cues.label: Mechanism
cues.headline: Three things change: shape, dtype, range
cues.narration: Three things change along the way: shape goes (H,W,C) to (C,H,W) to (1,C,H,W), dtype goes uint8 to float32, and range goes 0-255 to 0-1 to normalized.

cues.label: Implementation
cues.headline: The seven real lines, and how to test them
cues.narration: These are the seven real lines lit one at a time; the one formula is per-channel normalization, and you test with assert arr.shape == (1, 3, 896, 896) and arr.dtype == float32.

cues.label: Misconception
cues.headline: Separate the traps that look almost right
cues.narration: Separate the traps: PIL uses (Width, Height) not (H, W); dividing a uint8 array by 255 without first casting gives float64, which wastes twice the memory, so cast to float32 first; and normalize before you permute, not after.

cues.label: Synthesis
cues.headline: Seven stations, one tensor — now code it yourself
cues.narration: Seven stations, one tensor: open, resize, array, float, normalize, permute, batch. Preprocessing is the contract between messy real files and the model's exact numeric input.
